response.justification: The candidate is a versatile, experienced engineer with genuine developer-tooling instincts, but the role's core requirements are strong Java and Kotlin expertise with deep JVM knowledge and solid Spring experience — none of which his career demonstrates. His stack centers on TypeScript/Node.js and DLang; Java appears only peripherally, Kotlin and Spring not at all. His IDE experience (NI LabWindows/CVI) is real but brief, a decade old, and in C/C++ rather than the JVM ecosystem, making him a weak match despite his craftsmanship.
strengths: Direct IDE-internals experience building components for NI LabWindows/CVI
Authored developer tooling (Trial test runner, Fluent-Asserts) showing genuine interest in language tooling
Long track record across complex systems, including high-throughput low-latency services at Dunnhumby/Sociomantic
concerns: No demonstrated Kotlin experience and only marginal Java exposure, versus a hard requirement for strong expertise in both
No Spring, Quarkus, Hibernate, or other enterprise JVM framework experience
IDE-related work is brief, dated (2014-2015), and in the C ecosystem rather than JVM tooling

key_factors.factor: No Spring or enterprise framework experience
